interactive.rationale: Has some interactivity but the control surface is thin — only a single button/input drives the demo.

references.rationale: The 'see also' section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5175599392620544 — matches the feature.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles route through --bg/--text/--accent variables, no raw hex, and button overrides pair background + color correctly.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the keywords the spec/feature-index lede surfaces — covers the headline API surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Page only walks the happy path — there is no visible branch for failure, denial, or invalid input.

openQuestions.title: Broaden the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Currently a single control drives the demo. Adding a second axis (e.g. toggle, select, or slider) lets visitors explore a wider slice of the spec — particularly the failure modes.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently has no failure branch. Add a visible second branch that exercises an error path: unsupported browser, permission denied, malformed input, or quota-exceeded. The contrast is what teaches the spec.

summary: Mostly solid but with one structural gap (fail on the rubric). The major open question is the priority fix.
